description: Seeking 2–3 PhD students to join SEAL starting Fall 2027. Areas: multilingual NLP, AI safety, information integrity, algorithmic fairness. Strong preference for candidates with backgrounds in NLP, ML, HCI, computational linguistics, or related fields. International applicants encouraged.
howToApply: Apply to the CU Boulder Computer Science PhD program and mention SEAL / Jason Lucas in your statement. Email a CV to jason.lucas@colorado.edu before submitting.

audience: CU Boulder Master's and undergraduate students
description: CU Boulder Master's and undergraduate students interested in research experience are welcome. Prior coursework in ML/NLP is helpful but not required — motivation, consistency, and curiosity matter more. Opportunities range from thesis-length projects to semester-long research assistantships.
howToApply: Email jason.lucas@colorado.edu with (1) your resume, (2) a short paragraph on what draws you to SEAL's work, and (3) your available hours per week.

description: The lab is not currently recruiting postdocs. Future openings will be in multilingual AI safety, adversarial ML, and socio-technical evaluation. Check back or follow the lab's news for announcements.
howToApply: No active call. You are welcome to send a brief expression of interest to jason.lucas@colorado.edu — we'll keep it on file.
